retrieved_note: Verification pass 2026-05-25. Schedule of Bank Charges effective 1 January 2026 is now stored locally at data/card-requirements/raw/askari-bank/SOC-Jan-Jun-2026.pdf and was extracted directly via pdfplumber. All fee fields on credit and debit cards are taken verbatim from that PDF (Section E for debit, Section H for credit). Card pages on askaribank.com remain Cloudflare-WAF-blocked, so salary and relationship-balance thresholds still rely on the prior pilot extraction; the SOC itself does not publish per-card salary thresholds.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Annual fee Rs. 5,000 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section H.1.a.
Supplementary annual fee Rs. 2,500 verified from SOC Section H.1.g (Supplementary Card - Classic).
70% waiver wording is verbatim from SOC H.1.a.
Card replacement fee Rs. 900 (SOC H.7) is not stored on the requirements record but is the canonical replacement-fee source.

Shared public eligibility grid across Classic, Gold, and Platinum on the bank's Mastercard page (not in the SOC). Salaried-income threshold PKR 35,000 is the lowest band (GOP / Semi-Govt. / Autonomous permanent employees); approved-company and non-approved-company applicants sit at higher salary bands per the same page.
PKR 150,000 corresponds to the published SEB/SEP average-balance criterion shown alongside the salaried grid (product page, not SOC).
Maximum age 70 is the broadest envelope; FAQ on the bank's page splits the cap as 60 for permanent salaried, 65 for contractual salaried / pensioners, and 70 for SEB/SEP.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Annual fee Rs. 11,000 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section H.1.b.
Supplementary annual fee Rs. 5,500 verified from SOC Section H.1.h (Supplementary Card - Gold).
Eligibility is the shared Mastercard credit-card grid (product page, not SOC); there is no Gold-only product page.
Lowest published salaried threshold on that page is PKR 35,000 for GOP/Semi-Govt./Autonomous permanent employees; other salaried categories are higher.
SEB/SEP applicants qualify on PKR 150,000 average-balance criterion shown alongside the salary grid.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Annual fee Rs. 19,500 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section H.1.c.
Supplementary annual fee Rs. 10,000 verified from SOC Section H.1.i (Supplementary Card - Platinum).
Eligibility is the shared Mastercard credit-card grid (product page, not SOC); there is no Platinum-only product page.
PKR 35,000 salaried band is the lowest published row (GOP/Semi-Govt./Autonomous permanent); other salaried categories are higher.
PKR 150,000 balance applies to the SEB/SEP track of the same grid.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: 70% waiver on 50% utilization of total limit within 3 months of issuance or renewal.

notes: Annual fee Rs. 23,000 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section H.1.d. The 70% waiver wording is explicit in the SOC for the World tier (it applies, contrary to the 2024 SOC where World was charged flat).
Supplementary annual fee Rs. 12,500 verified from SOC Section H.1.j (Supplementary Card - World).

PKR 1,000,000 monthly salary floor confirmed by the data owner on 2026-05-25 — this is correct as a monthly salary threshold for the World tier. The SOC itself does not publish per-card salary thresholds, so this comes from the bank's product page.
SEB/SEP track on the same World product page publishes PKR 2.0 million annual income.
No separate public deposit-relationship threshold was found on the World card page.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: No annual or issuance fee if customer maintains the required minimum quarterly average balance; otherwise issuance and annual service fees apply per SOC.

notes: Issuance fee Rs. 3,000 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section E.1.a.i (Visa Chip Debit Card Basic & Supplementary - Classic).
Annual service fee Rs. 3,000 verified from SOC Section E.1.b.i.
SOC Section E.1 header explicitly states '(Basic & Supplementary)' for Visa Chip Debit Cards — supplementary annual fee uses the same Rs. 3,000 figure as basic.
Replacement fee Rs. 3,000 (SOC E.1.c.i) is not stored on the requirements record.
Card page states no minimum balance requirement for issuance or retention of the Visa Debit Card Classic.
salary field corrected from `0` to `null` per the methodology rule that silence on the bank page does not equal 'no requirement'.
Existing-account-required set to true because a debit card is issued against an existing Askari deposit account.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Free issuance and no annual fee when the customer maintains the required minimum quarterly average relationship balance; standard fee per SOC applies otherwise.

notes: Issuance fee Rs. 18,000 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section E.1.a.iv (Visa Chip Debit Card Basic & Supplementary - Signature).
Annual service fee Rs. 18,000 verified from SOC Section E.1.b.iv.
SOC Section E.1 header explicitly states '(Basic & Supplementary)' for Visa Chip Debit Cards — supplementary annual fee uses the same Rs. 18,000 figure as basic.
Replacement fee Rs. 18,000 (SOC E.1.c.iv) is not stored on the requirements record.

Eligibility is relationship-balance based, not salary based. PKR 5,000,000 minimum average quarterly current-account relationship for conventional banking comes from askaribank.com/ascend-priority. The Visa Signature Debit Card is the Ascend Priority debit card.
Issuance fee Rs. 2,400 verified directly from Askari Bank SOC effective 1 Jan 2026, Section E.3.a.ii (PayPak Debit Card Basic & Supplementary - Gold).
Annual service fee Rs. 2,400 verified from SOC Section E.3.b.ii.
SOC Section E.3 header explicitly states '(Basic & Supplementary)' for PayPak Debit Cards — supplementary annual fee uses the same Rs. 2,400 figure as basic.
Replacement fee Rs. 2,400 (SOC E.3.c.ii) is not stored on the requirements record.
PayPak Gold debit issuance is account-linked rather than salary-gated; the public card page does not publish a salary or balance requirement of its own.

Linked-account inheritance: Askari Asaan Current and Asaan Saving accounts both advertise PKR 100 opening amount and no minimum-balance requirement, and both expose PayPak debit card issuance on their official product pages.
salary field corrected from `0` to `null` per the methodology rule.

gaps: Debit-card pages do not publish age criteria; age fields remain null.
Account-linked debit cards inherit their effective eligibility from the linked account product, but the card page itself does not state a salary requirement.

Mastercard credit-card eligibility on the bank's page is published as a per-applicant-category grid; the pilot collapses the grid to a single lowest-band threshold (GOP/Semi-Govt./Autonomous permanent at PKR 35,000 / SEB/SEP at PKR 150,000 average balance) — refinement by applicant category is still outstanding.
Per-card salary and balance thresholds come from product pages (still Cloudflare-WAF-blocked); the Jan 2026 SOC is now in repo and authoritative for all fee fields.

SOC contains additional card variants not yet in the pilot: Visa Chip Debit Gold (Rs. 3,500) and Platinum (Rs. 8,000); UnionPay Chip Debit Classic (Rs. 2,200) and Gold (Rs. 2,400); PayPak Silver (Rs. 1,700); FCY Debit Card (USD 15); Corporate Mastercard Credit (Rs. 4,800); Awami Mastercard Credit (Rs. 2,300, existing portfolio only); Askari PIA Co-Brand Classic/Gold/Platinum (Rs. 5,000 / 8,000 / 10,000).
